calendar.matters:
Motion To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion To Set Aside Dismissal.

The unopposed motion to set aside dismissal is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2024 Dismissal

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Oct-15-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. LYNDA JACOBS, Esq. of Hemming & Associates, P.C. (tel: 714-443-0713) for the Defendant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO DEFENDANT DAVID COHEN.

Matter discussed. The Court holds a discussion regarding the trial date. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court denies the motion for withdrawal of attorney of record without prejudice to file a substitution of attorney, as the trial is set for November 12, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jul-29-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Off calendar. The Carr declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 439. Defendant is ordered to comply with the code.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-12-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted
Off Calendar May-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The unopposed motion is granted.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to jpdeol@dhillonlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
